page: Microsoft Azure E64bds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the EBDSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The memory configuration provides 512.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage is bundled with 2458 GB of capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls in the average tier, multi-core execution is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for large block reads and writes is also strong, registering at 303.98 GB/sec and 134.85 GB/sec respectively. Database operations and Redis SET operations demonstrate strong performance, as does static web serving throughput at 55.1 GB/sec.

Tradeoffs are evident in workloads requiring hardware acceleration or large-scale sequential processing. The server lacks a dedicated GPU, and large-scale LLM inference is a bottleneck, with text generation for a 70B model scoring in the weak tier at 2.78 tokens per second. However, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong multi-core performance make this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and large-scale multi-threaded data processing.
description: Microsoft Azure E64bds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the EBDSv5 family, powered by a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 2458 GB of local storage. Performance benchmarks show average single-core execution but strong multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for multi-threaded decompression, PassMark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth is also strong, with large block reads reaching 303.98 GB/sec. The system excels in database operations and Redis SET performance, though it exhibits weak throughput for large LLM inference due to the lack of GPU acceleration. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for memory-optimized workloads, relational databases, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64bds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64bds_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, built for database and multi-core workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server with dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-capacity local storage, and strong multi-core performance for database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
512 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio
2458 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark results
Weak performance for large LLM inference due to lack of GPU acceleration
Best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded workloads
